subject: Anthropology
title: Monumental Architecture
description: A quick, practical guide to Monumental Architecture.

hook.text: The Great Pyramid took 20 years and 100,000 workers. It wasn't efficiency — it was a display of absolute power.

buildup.text: Monumental architecture — pyramids, ziggurats, temples — served to display the power of rulers and religious systems.

discovery.text: Building these structures required massive labor, engineering knowledge, and centralized authority to coordinate it all.

twist.text: The laborers weren't always slaves. Egyptian pyramid builders were fed well and buried with honor near the monuments.

climax.text: Monuments are power made visible. They tell future generations: someone here commanded enough people to build this.

quiz.question: What does monumental architecture reveal?
options: That ancient people had nothing to do
Centralized power and organized labor systems
That only slaves built large structures
